reason: Successfully verified that the agent can use the GitHub tool to fetch the authenticated user's GitHub username, with proper account access request and permission handling.
steps: Navigated to http://localhost:47891 — Page loaded successfully with SuperAgent application and sidebar showing available agents.
Found the "QA-20260512-221604-p4or" agent in the sidebar and clicked it — Agent detail page loaded, showing agent name and status indicator.
Verified agent status is "running" or "idle" — Agent status confirmed as "idle" (visible in status indicator at top of page).
Sent message: "Use the GitHub tool to check who I am. Tell me my GitHub username." — Message was typed into the input field and sent successfully, transitioning to chat view.
A GitHub account access request card appeared asking to grant GitHub account access — Request Connected Account card displayed with GitHub option checked and "Allow Access" button ready.

Clicked "Allow Access (1)" button to grant GitHub access — Permission granted, agent resumed processing. A second permission dialog appeared asking "Allow get the authenticated user?" for the GitHub API call.
Clicked "Always allow user" on the permission dialog — Additional permissions granted for reading user profile data.
Waited up to 4 minutes for the response — Agent completed processing in approximately 37 seconds, successfully executed three tool calls:

Verified the response includes a GitHub username and took a screenshot — Response clearly shows: "Your GitHub username is yiw190." Screenshot captured showing the complete conversation with all tool cards and the final result.
